AI.name: Ada Lovelace
AI.preview: Spent the morning reading the Llama 3 paper and reproducing the rotary positional embedding math by hand. This afternoon I want to pair on a tiny GPT to make sure I really get attention.
AI.suggested_message: Hey Ada! Saw your check-in about reproducing rotary positional embeddings — I have been wanting to dig into that too. Want to pair on a tiny GPT this afternoon?

AI.name: Grace Hopper
AI.preview: Building an evals harness for a small RAG project. Today: write a regression test that catches when retrieval starts pulling the wrong section headings.
AI.suggested_message: Hi Grace! Loved your check-in about RAG evals — I’m fighting the same retrieval-drift problem. Coffee chat sometime this week?

Rust.name: Linus T.
Rust.preview: Porting a small game-of-life from C to Rust to learn ownership. Today’s yak: figuring out why my borrow checker is unhappy with a callback in winit.
Rust.suggested_message: Hey Linus! I have battled the same winit closure-borrow issue — happy to pair if you want a second pair of eyes.

Rust.name: Margaret H.
Rust.preview: Started writing a tiny embedded-style allocator in Rust to learn unsafe. Plan: read Phil Opp’s blog OS posts and steal his bump allocator.
Rust.suggested_message: Hi Margaret! I am also working through Phil Opp’s OS series — would love to swap notes on the bump allocator section.

Web.name: Tim B-L.
Web.preview: First week! Building a personal site without a framework — just plain HTML, CSS, and a sprinkle of JS. Today: get view transitions working between pages.
Web.suggested_message: Welcome Tim! View Transitions is a fun rabbit hole — I have a tiny demo with same-document transitions, happy to share.

Web.name: Radia P.
Web.preview: Hacking on a self-hosted RSS reader. Today: deduplicate items across feeds that re-publish the same post under different GUIDs.
Web.suggested_message: Hi Radia! I went down the same dedup rabbit hole — ended up using a SimHash on the title + first paragraph. Want to compare notes?

Music.name: Hedy L.
Music.preview: Writing a tiny step sequencer in Web Audio. Today’s goal: get swing timing to feel right (currently sounds robotic).
Music.suggested_message: Hey Hedy! Web Audio + groove is one of my favourite things — happy to swap the swing-timing tricks I’ve picked up.

Other.name: Edsger D.
Other.preview: Giving a talk on Friday about why I think property-based tests beat example tests for parser code. Today: building the slide deck and a short live demo.
Other.suggested_message: Hi Edsger! Looking forward to the talk Friday — would you want a dry run with someone who has been writing PBTs for parsers?
